summary: Cathy O'Neil announces the launch of her data platform class, sharing educational resources she's prepared. She provides links to the course syllabus on GitHub and an iPython notebook demonstrating basic statistics in Python. The notebook uses the controversial Facebook Emotion study as a case study for understanding statistical significance. She encourages readers to follow along by installing Anaconda and promises future blog posts about guest lecturers.
key_points: New data platform class launching with open syllabus available on GitHub
Teaching materials include iPython notebook for hands-on learning of basic statistics
Uses Facebook Emotion study as real-world example of statistically significant but tiny differences
Course will feature guest lecturers whose talks may be blogged about
money_quotes: I prepared an iPython notebook showing how to do basic statistics in python, and culminating in an attempt to understand what a statistically significant but tiny difference means, in the context of the Facebook Emotion study.
key_insight: Understanding the difference between statistical significance and practical significance is crucial, especially when analyzing controversial studies like Facebook's emotion manipulation experiment.
